Title: PagSAMDC4a-Mediated Polyamine Synthesis Regulate Vessel Differentiation Under Drought Stress Conditions in Poplar

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SAMDC family; decarboxylase enzyme in polyamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Pro35S (constitutive) for overexpression; native PagSAMDC4a promoter for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in phloem and cambium zones of stems; strong in young stems and leaves; vascular tissues and roots; drought inducible
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in phloem and cambium/wood-forming tissues; induced by 200 mM mannitol in stems, by drought in stems at 3 d and leaves at 7 d
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; mannitol treatment; exogenous polyamines; MGBG (SAMDC inhibitor); H2O2; KI
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Decarboxylated SAM supply for spermidine/spermine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream committed/control step supplying aminopropyl donor for downstream polyamine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates S-adenosyl-L-methionine to produce decarboxylated SAM, the aminopropyl donor used for spermidine and spermine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme supported by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances drought tolerance, contributes to redox homeostasis, and promotes adaptive xylem vessel differentiation under water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes polyamine accumulation, decreases xylem vessel size and density, lowers H2O2 level, and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PagSAMDC4a is the only SAMDC member specifically enriched in wood-forming tissues and induced by drought. 2. Overexpression increased spermidine and spermine contents, whereas CRISPR knockout decreased them. 3. Elevated PagSAMDC4a reduced xylem width, vessel lumen area and vessel number, while knockout caused larger and more numerous vessels. 4. PagSAMDC4a overexpression improved drought tolerance, with higher leaf relative water content and lower electrolyte leakage, MDA and H2O2 under drought. 5. H2O2 treatment rescued the small-vessel phenotype of overexpression lines and KI reduced vessel size in knockout lines, linking PagSAMDC4a-controlled polyamines to vessel differentiation via H2O2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; promoter::GUS assay; RT-qPCR; polyamine metabolite profiling; stem cross-section histology; drought phenotyping; H2O2/KI chemical rescue; MGBG inhibitor treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: AspWood expression database analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SPDS; SPMS; PagRBOH02; PagRBOH06; PagRBOH10; PagCSD2a; PagCSD2b; PagCSD3a; PagCSD3b; PagMSD1/2b
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced xylem width and smaller vessel size may alter wood anatomy; no significant difference in plant height or basal diameter was observed in tested lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering drought-tolerant poplar with altered wood properties; overexpression or activation in vascular tissues may improve resistance to water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper positions PagSAMDC4a as controlling vessel differentiation indirectly through modulation of ROS, especially H2O2, rather than by direct structural roles in xylem development.
